name: Manage catch certificates (list + upload more?)

heading: Manage catch certificates

structure.text: Do you need to upload more catch certificates?
Do you need to upload more catch certificates?

govukComponents.usage: Section caption 'Documents' above the H1; 'Attachment 1 of 1' caption inside each attachment card (govuk-caption-m)
govuk-heading-xl = page H1 'Manage catch certificates'; govuk-heading-m = 'Catch certificates uploaded' H2; govuk-heading-s = attachment filename H3 and inline 'Reference:' H2
Wraps the 'Do you need to upload more catch certificates?' radio group; legend rendered as page heading (govuk-fieldset__heading is an h1)
Yes / No radio group controlling whether the user loops back to upload another catch certificate; not rendered inline (stacked)
Two side-by-side primary buttons: 'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue' (neither uses govuk-button--secondary)
Attachment filename link (opens in new tab), 'View or amend details', 'Remove', 'Cancel and return to hub'
Paragraph wrapping the 'Cancel and return to hub' link
Page layout; one-half columns split flag-state vs species-count inside the attachment card

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke card container that renders one uploaded catch certificate: 'Attachment N of M' caption, filename link (opens in new tab), 'Reference:' value, a two-column row of 'Flag state' and 'N species added', and 'View or amend details' / 'Remove' action links.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom IPAFFS widget, not a Design System component. Repeats per uploaded certificate. The new app should render each certificate with a standard component rather than a bespoke div.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary card (govuk-summary-card) with a Summary list and card actions, or a Summary list per certificate. A Task list is a weaker fit since these are completed items with edit/remove actions.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom link styling on the attachment filename link — removes the default underline and adds a hover highlight instead.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Overrides the standard govuk-link appearance (underlined by default). Deviates from GOV.UK link accessibility guidance.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Use a plain govuk-link (keep the default underline). No custom CSS needed.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The page has both a govuk-heading-xl H1 ('Manage catch certificates') AND a fieldset legend rendered as an H1 via govuk-fieldset__heading ('Do you need to upload more catch certificates?').
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Two H1s on a single page is an accessibility anti-pattern. It arises from combining a page-title H1 with the standard 'legend as page heading' radios pattern (which also emits an H1). The list part and the question part are effectively two pages fused into one.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Split into a list page (H1 = 'Manage catch certificates') and a separate question page, OR demote the legend to a normal fieldset legend (not an H1) so there is a single page H1.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 51 (a11y tree shows two level=1 headings)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern:
Reference:

followed by an inline value span
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Uses a heading element as an inline label prefix ('Reference:') sitting on the same line as the certificate reference value.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A heading is being used for inline labelling, not document structure — semantically incorrect and confuses the heading outline.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Represent the reference as a row in a Summary list (key 'Reference', value 'CatchCertificateRef-...'), not a heading.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Two govuk-button elements with no govuk-button--secondary
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: 'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue' render as two equally-weighted primary buttons side by side.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: GOV.UK guidance is one primary action per page; a secondary/lower-priority action should use govuk-button--secondary or be a link. Two primary buttons compete for attention.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Make 'Save and continue' the single primary button and demote 'Save and return to hub' to govuk-button--secondary or a link.

fields.name: upload-catch-certificates
fields.label: Do you need to upload more catch certificates?
fields.conditionalOn: IUU / fishery-products (CHED-P fish) journey only. On 'Yes' the journey loops back to the attach page (file upload #fileUpload → AttachCatchCertificatePage) to upload another catch certificate; on 'No' it proceeds to the next step (Latest Health Certificate on medium risk). The page object accesses these radios by the accessible group name 'Do you need to upload more catch certificates?' (confirming the legend text) and the loop-vs-proceed behaviour is exercised directly by the workflow.
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 51 (Yes, value=yes, id=upload-catch-certificates-yes), action 64 (No, value=no, id=upload-catch-certificates-no); corroborated by page-objects/notification/ManageCatchCertificatesPage.ts:11-13 (moreUploadsGroup = getByRole('group', { name: 'Do you need to upload more catch certificates?' })), :15-17 (radioMoreCatchCertificateNeeded = Yes), :19-21 (radioMoreCatchCertificateNotNeeded = No); loop driven at workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:903-907 (index>0 → check Yes → Save and continue → back to attach page), exit at :927-928 (No → Save and continue past the catch-cert step)

fields.name: (attachment card action)
fields.label: Add details
fields.conditionalOn: Variant state of the per-certificate action link NOT seen in our trace corpus: shown when a certificate has been uploaded/attached but its details (reference, date of issue, flag state, species) have not yet been captured. The test clicks this link immediately after attaching a file, before any details exist. Once details are saved, the same slot renders as 'View or amend details' (the state our trace captured). Links to the add-catch-certificate-details form.
fields.evidence: page-objects/notification/ManageCatchCertificatesPage.ts:6-8 (linkAddDetails = getByRole('link', { name: 'Add details' })); driven at workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:914 (pages.manageCatchCertificates.linkAddDetails.first().click() straight after uploading the file at :910-911). Note .first() is used because with N un-detailed uploads there could be multiple 'Add details' links; the workflow always details them one at a time immediately after each upload

fields.name: (attachment card action)
fields.label: View or amend details
fields.conditionalOn: One per uploaded catch certificate that already has its details captured; links to /add-catch-certificate-details/attachment/{attachmentId}. This is the 'details present' state of the same action slot that renders 'Add details' when details are missing.

fields.name: (attachment card action)
fields.label: Remove
fields.conditionalOn: One per uploaded catch certificate; links to /upload/.../{attachmentId}/notification/remove-attachment

validationMessages.trigger: Submitting 'Save and continue' with no radio selected is the likely validation, but no error state was captured in this trace (the radio was always selected before submit).
validationMessages.evidence: not observed — this trace (db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d) has 0 errors; no error-summary rendered on this page in any snapshot examined

openQuestions: Is the 'Do you need to upload more catch certificates?' radio group server-side required? No HTML required attribute was set and no validation error was captured (this is a clean happy-path trace), and NO test asserts any validation message for this page — so the 'Select an option'-style message remains a genuine gap, not merely un-traced. Needs an error-state trace or the frontend template to confirm.

Our trace captured only the single-certificate state ('Attachment 1 of 1'). The QA workflow's DEFAULT_FISH_CONFIG uploads catchCertificateCount: 2 (ched-p-workflows.ts:825), and the manage page loops (radioMoreCatchCertificateNeeded → re-attach) to accrue N attachment cards. So the page must render a repeating list of N cards ('Attachment 1 of N' … 'Attachment N of N'), not just one. The new app must support the multi-certificate list, not assume a single upload.

The per-certificate action link has TWO states on the same slot: 'Add details' (details not yet captured — inferred from ManageCatchCertificatesPage.ts:6-8, driven at ched-p-workflows.ts:914) and 'View or amend details' (details present — confirmed in the trace). The rebuild should model this as one 'complete/edit' affordance whose label reflects completion state.
The page fuses a certificate LIST (with per-item Add/View details + Remove) and a Yes/No question onto one URL, producing two H1s. The rebuild should decide whether these are one page or two.

Attachment card fields observed (filename, Reference, Flag state, '1 species added') are dynamic per-certificate content — flag state and species come from the earlier 'add catch certificate details' page. The '1 species added' count and 'Flag state' summary are derived, not entered here. Confirmed source controls on that details page: reference = #catch-certificate-reference-1, date of issue = #date-of-issue-{day,month,year}-1, flag state = combobox 'Flag state of catching vessel(s)' (autocomplete, value 'France'), species = 'select all species' checkbox #select-all-checkbox-1 (page-objects/notification/AddCatchCertificateDetailsPage.ts:7-30) — so '1 species added' is the count of species selected there. The downstream Review page asserts each certificate row shows its reference, flag state ('France') and attachment filename (ched-p-notification.spec.ts:210-217), corroborating these three as the load-bearing per-certificate data.

Attachment-card FILENAME is not a fixed string. The trace captured a single card reading 'example.png', but the QA workflow re-uploads the SAME source file (resources/file-upload/example.png) for every certificate, and the tests assert the review-page filenames match /example(\(\d+\))?\.png/ — i.e. the app de-duplicates repeated uploads as example.png, example(1).png, example(2).png, … (ched-p-notification.spec.ts:215-216, comment: 'Re-uploading the same file produces example.png, example(1).png, example(2).png, etc.'). The rebuild must handle duplicate-filename disambiguation across multiple uploads. confidence: inferred (test assertion, not observed in our single-cert trace).

COVERAGE GAP: this page is NOT listed in tests/accessibility/ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts (grep for 'manage catch' / 'catch certif' returns nothing there) — so no axe/a11y assertion covers it, despite the trace showing it emits TWO h1 elements and uses a heading ('Reference:') as an inline label. The a11y anti-patterns flagged in nonStandardPatterns are therefore un-caught by the QA suite.
URL confirmed as /notification/vnet/protected/notifications/{chedReference}/manage-catch-certificates (the evidence-brief inferred '/catch-certificate/manage', which is wrong).

The notification-reference banner 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979 - CHEDP' shows this IUU journey is filed under a CHED-P (CHEDP) draft reference in IPAFFS — consistent with the brief that fish IUU is filed as CHED-P.

The page object drives only 'Save and continue' (btnSaveAndContinue); the trace-confirmed secondary actions 'Save and return to hub', 'Cancel and return to hub' and 'Remove' are never exercised by the tests, so their behaviour/validation is untested by QA (trace-confirmed for existence only).
